response_body_utf8: {"status":"ok","message-type":"work","message-version":"1.0.0","message":{"indexed":{"date-parts":[[2026,5,9]],"date-time":"2026-05-09T02:11:07Z","timestamp":1778292667245,"version":"3.51.4"},"posted":{"date-parts":[[2026]]},"group-title":"SSRN","reference-count":0,"publisher":"Elsevier BV","content-domain":{"domain":[],"crossmark-restriction":false},"short-container-title":[],"abstract":"<jats:p>Image translation between daytime and nighttime conditions is a critical problem in computational imaging. This translation enables improved perception in low-illumination environments, such as nighttime driving. However, existing learning-based approaches often require large paired datasets or rely purely on data-driven priors, limiting their ability to generalize to real-world nighttime imaging conditions and capture underlying image formation processes.In this paper, we propose a physics-informed image translation framework that synthesizes realistic nighttime images from daytime inputs by incorporating differentiable degradation modules inspired by radiometric imaging principles. The proposed framework models the important low-light effects, including illumination attenuation, illumination-dependent reflectance variation, atmospheric scattering, and spatially varying illumination fields. These components are embedded within a generator architecture comprising convolutional, attention-based, and diffusion-inspired refinement blocks, enabling both structural consistency and realistic degradation synthesis.The model is trained end-to-end using adversarial and perceptual losses. Compared to purely data-driven approaches, the proposed framework improves interpretability by explicitly parameterizing degradation processes while allowing them to adapt to real-world image statistics.Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method produces nighttime images that are perceptually and distributionally similar to real scenes, as measured by LPIPS and FID. Furthermore, the translated images improve performance in downstream object detection tasks. From a computational imaging perspective, the proposed framework can be viewed as a learnable, differentiable forward model for nighttime image formation, explicitly modeling main radiometric effects within a deep generative architecture.<\/jats:p>","DOI":"10.2139\/ssrn.6737115","type":"posted-content","created":{"date-parts":[[2026,5,9]],"date-time":"2026-05-09T01:40:10Z","timestamp":1778290810000},"source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":0,"title":["Physics-Informed Nighttime Image Synthesis From Daytime Scenes Using Differentiable Degradation Modeling"],"prefix":"10.2139","author":[{"given":"Ravindra","family":"Gautam","sequence":"first","affiliation":[],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]},{"given":"Anirban","family":"Dasgupta","sequence":"additional","affiliation":[],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]},{"given":"Anurag","family":"Gupta","sequence":"additional","affiliation":[],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]}],"member":"78","container-title":[],"original-title":[],"deposited":{"date-parts":[[2026,5,9]],"date-time":"2026-05-09T01:40:10Z","timestamp":1778290810000},"score":1,"resource":{"primary":{"URL":"https:\/\/www.ssrn.com\/abstract=6737115"}},"subtitle":[],"short-title":[],"issued":{"date-parts":[[2026]]},"references-count":0,"URL":"https:\/\/doi.org\/10.2139\/ssrn.6737115","relation":{},"subject":[],"published":{"date-parts":[[2026]]},"subtype":"preprint"}}
